patches_reviewed.subject: Re: [PATCH v5 2/3] cxl/core/region: move dax region device logic into region_dax.c
patches_reviewed.patch_summary: Move of dax region device logic from cxl/core/region to region_dax.c

review_comments.author: Gregory Price
review_comments.summary: Questioned whether the code move is purely non-functional and if it would cause issues with the original code. Suggested that the author double-check their work.

review_comments.author: Dan Williams
review_comments.summary: Confirmed that the moved code does not exist in mainline, thus making Gregory's concerns about functional changes unfounded.
review_comments.raw_body:
Gregory Price wrote:
> On Fri, Apr 10, 2026 at 05:02:00PM -0700, Anisa Su wrote:
> > > +
> > > +	cxlr_dax->hpa_range.start = p->res->start;
> > > +	cxlr_dax->hpa_range.end = p->res->end;
> > > +
> > > +	dev = &cxlr_dax->dev;
> > > +	cxlr_dax->cxlr = cxlr;
> > 	cxlr->cxlr_dax = cxlr_dax;
> > 
> > Running into segfaults without this ^
> 
> This should have been a purely non-functional change (the code is just
> moving from A to B without changes).
> 
> That would mean the original code should run into this too unless I
> mucked up the move.

I do not think you mucked anything up ->cxlr_dax does not exist in mainline.

patches_reviewed.subject: Re: [RFC PATCH 00/20] DCD: Remove support for sparse regions & add tags

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH] sunrpc: start cache request seqno at 1 to fix netlink GET_REQS

version_history.subject: [PATCH] sunrpc: start cache request seqno at 1 to fix netlink GET_REQS

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the sunrpc cache where requests were being silently skipped when the sequence number was 0, causing netlink-based GET_REQS to return incorrect results and leading to NFS4ERR_DELAY hangs. The fix is to start the next sequence number at 1 instead of 0, ensuring that all requests have a sequence number greater than or equal to 1 and pass the snapshot filter.

patches_reviewed.subject: Re: Re: [PATCH 0/2] fuse: Fix possible memleak at startup with immediate teardown
patches_reviewed.patch_summary: This patch fixes a possible memory leak in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module by introducing preemptive checking at registration time to prevent races between registration and abort operations. The patch addresses two potential hangs that can occur when there are concurrent registrations and aborts, one due to a race between registration and queue teardown, and another due to a race between ring creation and abort. To fix these issues, the patch introduces additional checks and synchronization mechanisms to ensure that resources are properly released before they are reused or deallocated.

review_comments.author: Horst Birthelmer
review_comments.summary: Reviewer Horst Birthelmer agrees with the original patch's analysis of the race condition, but suggests checking the fc->connected state under the fc lock before creating the ring to prevent a hang on ring creation vs abort races.
review_comments.raw_body:
It does align with what I have seen, even though my testcase only tested
the teardown when the fuse server ran into a problem.
You expanded that quite a bit ;-)

review_comments.author: Bernd Schubert
review_comments.summary:
Bernd Schubert expressed skepticism about the existence of a registration time leak, stating that he doesn't see any leak even with the additional patch to tear down entries through IO_URING_F_CANCEL.

Reviewer Bernd Schubert pointed out that the described memory leak does not occur in io-uring, but rather is caused by a missing free of 'queue->ent_in_userspace' in fuse_uring_destruct, and suggested simplifying the fix to just freeing the entries in the list without additional complexity.
review_comments.raw_body:
I need to go through the other messages, but I still do not see any
registration time leak. At least not with the additional patch we have
here to tear down entries through IO_URING_F_CANCEL


Sorry, besides also looking into ublk now (for main work), also in
progress to fix an issue with reduced queues and also still on the
libfuse part of sync-init....

---

IO-uring sends IO_URING_F_CANCEL for every registred command. We never
had a leak you describe. Upstream has a leak because it does not free
'queue->ent_in_userspace' in fuse_uring_destruct. I'm fine with the
addition in fuse_uring_cancel() (although the just freeing the entries
in the list is much simpler and race free).

Please let's not make it anymore complex.


Thanks,
Bernd
